title: Letters reveal climber's last message to mum before fatal Everest ascent

input.lead: Relatives of the Cheshire-born mountaineer discover letters Geroge Mallory wrote to his mother
input.summary: Relatives of the Cheshire-born mountaineer discover letters Geroge Mallory wrote to his mother
input.evidence_text: Legendary mountaineer George Mallory wrote to his mother to say "the ink has begun to freeze" before he disappeared on Mount Everest in 1924, newly-discovered letters have revealed The writings record his first sight of the world's tallest peak, which he described as "a terrible formidable mountain", and include efforts to reassure his family with the words "I shall take every care I can" Mallory, who was born in the Cheshire village of Mobberley, died during the expedition in 1924 and his body was missing for 75 years His great-nephew Bill Newton Dunn, 84, said his wife had found the letters, "a precious family archive", in a box secured with a pink ribbon "The letters mean a lot to me because they mention my grandmother [Mallory's sister] Mary, so it's a precious family archive and these are historical documents, so its important that they are well-looked after when they are sold," he said The letters, alongside photographs and an ice axe thought to have been used by Mallory were auctioned off by Sotheby's earlier this week

title: Warburton Toll Bridge fine issued to driver 270 miles away

input.lead: A driver said he was left "perturbed" after receiving a fine for using a toll bridge despite having never been near it
input.summary: A driver said he was left "perturbed" after receiving a fine for using a toll bridge despite having never been near it
input.evidence_text: Warburton Toll Bridge fine issued to driver 270 miles away - BBC News Image caption, The Warburton Toll Bridge crosses the Manchester Ship Canal By Kaleigh Watterson Cheshire political reporter Published 46 minutes ago A driver said he was left "perturbed" after receiving a fine for crossing a toll bridge more than 270 miles from his home despite insisting he had never been near it. Graham Parsons, from Plymouth, Devon, received an unpaid toll charge for using the Warburton Toll Bridge, which links Cheshire and Greater Manchester. His case is one of a number raised by motorists who have complained about the bridge's payment and enforcement system. Peel Ports said there had been "some genuine customer experience issues", but the evidence did "not indicate a systemic failure of the system". Image caption, Graham Parsons received a charge - despite living more than 270 miles away from the bridge The bridge previously cost 12p a crossing, but the charge was increased to £1 following refurbishment works in recent years . Manned toll booths were removed and replaced with an automatic number plate recognition (ANPR) camera system. Mr Parsons told BBC Politics North West he received an unpaid toll charge in early June for a crossing in March. "I put my Sherlock Holmes hat on because I didn't realise that I'd ever been near the bridge," he said. "And in fact, I had never been near the bridge because it's 271 miles from where I live. So I was quite perturbed and annoyed." He appealed and discovered the camera had misread his registration plate, which differed by one character from the vehicle photographed. Two weeks later, he received a second letter relating to another crossing in April. Mr Parsons then submitted a subject access request to parking operator Excel Parking, after

title: North West safety warnings as two people die in open water

input.lead: Water safety groups reiterate warnings after recent deaths in reservoirs in Greater Manchester

input.evidence_text: North West safety warnings as two people die in open water - BBC News Image source, Steven Haslington/Geograph Image caption, Reservoirs are "extremely dangerous and are not safe places to swim", police said By Lynette Horsburgh North West Published 11 July 2026 Safety campaigners are warnings of the dangers of open water and reservoir swimming after the deaths of two men in just over 24 hours in Greater Manchester. The body of an 18-year-old man was pulled from Dovestone Reservoir, Oldham, on Friday, while a 49-year-old man's body was recovered from Sykes Reservoir, in Stockport, on Thursday. Matt Black, a crew manager with Lancashire Fire and Rescue Service, said if swimmers got into difficulties in reserviors, there was often "no escape out of it". The warning comes after coastguards and RNLI teams in the north-west of England reported a spike in incidents as people flock to the seaside amid the heatwave. Image source, HM Coastguard Wirral Image caption, HM Coastguard's Wirral team also issued a warning to people not to try and cool in open water during the hot weather Greater Manchester Police urged people not to be tempted to cool off in open water as high temperatures continue across the North West this week. The force said reservoirs were "extremely dangerous and are not safe places to swim". "Reservoirs are clearly signposted and swimming is prohibited, yet incidents continue to occur," it said. "Even on the hottest days, the water temperature can remain much colder than expected, often only reaching around 12°C to 16°C. "Cold water shock can happen quickly, affecting your breathing, movement and ability to stay afloat. title: How Geoff Hurst became England's World Cup hat-trick hero

input.lead: Looking back at the era-defining hat-trick in 1966 by Geoff Hurst who was born in Ashton-under-Lyne
input.summary: Looking back at the era-defining hat-trick in 1966 by Geoff Hurst who was born in Ashton-under-Lyne
input.evidence_text: In the final minute of extra time, as England battled West Germany in the 1966 World Cup final, Bobby Moore launched a long ball to an unmarked Sir Geoff Hurst The striker from Ashton-under-Lyne was exhausted as the end of the extra time drew near, and decided he was "just going to hit the ball as hard as I can" "And of course, I mis-hit it, and it flew in", Sir Geoff told BBC Radio 4 in 2018, while looking back at the final goal of his hat-trick in England's now iconic 4-2 victory at Wembley As the Three Lions prepare to face off against Norway later, BBC Radio Manchester has been looking back at Sir Geoff's memories of his path to the final and his era-defining hat-trick 60 years ago Sir Geoff was born in 1941 in Ashton-under-Lyne, where he grew up with his mother Evelyn and father Charlie, who was also a professional footballer Charlie played for Oldham Athletic, Rochdale and Mossley before the family moved South for him to join Chelmsford City

title: Why voting method change matters in mayoral poll

input.lead: Why the Supplementary Vote may allow voters to vote with their heads and their hearts
input.summary: Why the Supplementary Vote may allow voters to vote with their heads and their hearts
input.evidence_text: The Labour Party could benefit most from the return of the Supplementary Votes (SV) system in the Greater Manchester mayoral race, a polling expert has said Voters in the region head to the polls on 30 July and will be able to select a first and second choice of candidate as SV comes into replace the first-past-the-post (FPTP) system used at the last election A number of candidates have already declared for the contest , triggered after Andy Burnham's successful campaign to become the MP of Makerfield, leaving the post he has held since 2017 vacant Prof Jonathan Tonge of the University of Liverpool said Labour could pick up second preference votes from Greens and Lib Dems, while Reform could be less likely to see Restore voters put them as second preference due to "bad blood" between the party leaders The system was used in the first two mayoral elections, but was scrapped by the last Conservative government in favour of FPTP system, before it was reintroduced again by Labour Under SCV, candidates need to win more than 50% of the vote in the first round to be come mayor or the top two candidates go into a run-off where second preference votes from eliminated candidates are counted

title: What is the Greater Manchester mayor and what do they do?

input.lead: Greater Manchester goes to the polls on 30 July, but what powers does the region's mayor hold?
input.summary: Greater Manchester goes to the polls on 30 July, but what powers does the region's mayor hold?
input.evidence_text: Andy Burnham's victory in the Makerfield by-election means he has to step down as Greater Manchester mayor Burnham has been mayor since the role was first created in 2017 and the race to become his successor will be fiercely contested by all involved But what is the role they will be fighting for? The role of mayor was created as part of a law passed in 2016, one of a number of big pieces of devolution legislation brought in by the Conservatives after taking power in 2010 In a 2012 referendum, Manchester rejected having a directly-elected city mayor, along with eight other major cities, but the decision to have a regional or metropolitan mayor was directly negotiated between the government and the 10 Greater Manchester councils Andy Burnham, who left Parliament to stand for the role, was chosen as Labour's candidate for the first mayoral election in 2017 and won, taking 63.4% of the vote

title: How do I register to vote in the May 2026 elections?

input.lead: Here’s what you need to know about how you register to vote and when
input.summary: Here’s what you need to know about how you register to vote and when
input.evidence_text: Click to expand How do I register to vote? Millions of voters across England, Scotland and Wales are heading to the polls on 7 May. Depending where you are, you might be voting to elect your local council members, a new mayor, members of the Welsh Senedd, or Scottish Parliament But if you're going to vote, you need to make sure you are registered. The deadline for registering to vote has passed but there's still time to apply for a postal or proxy vote Here's what you need to know about how and when you register to vote Registering only takes a few minutes if you do it online. It’s the same process whether you plan to vote in person at a polling station, by post, or through a proxy voter Go to the gov.uk website and fill out the form. You'll need your National Insurance number and current address details to hand If you can't provide your NI number you can still complete the online form but you'll need to provide documents like a passport or utility bill to your local electoral registration office before you're properly registered

title: Teenager dies at Dovestone reservoir

input.lead: Police say the man was found 'unresponsive' in the water on Friday evening
input.summary: Police say the man was found 'unresponsive' in the water on Friday evening
input.evidence_text: Teenager dies at Dovestone reservoir - BBC News Image source, David Dixon/Geograph Image caption, Police are warning people not to enter open waters By Paul Burnell North West Published 11 July 2026 The body of an 18-year-old man has been pulled from a Greater Manchester reservoir. Greater Manchester Police said emergency services were to Dovestone Reservoir, Oldham at about 19:00 BST on Friday. Police in Oldham posted on social media , external that the man was found "unresponsive" in the water and "despite best efforts of medical professionals, he was sadly pronounced dead at the scene". Det Insp Steven Horton said: "We have tragically recovered the body of an 18-year-old man from the water and are conducting extensive enquiries to establish the full circumstances." He added: "Our thoughts remain with his family and friends during this incredibly difficult time. "We strongly encourage people to stay safe and refrain from entering large bodies of water including reservoirs, rivers, canals, or ponds" "Your safety is our priority. Please look after yourselves and enjoy the weather safely." A scene remains in place and enquiries are ongoing. title: Manchester United's new stadium to have fewer car parks

input.lead: Fans of Manchester United are being told to use public transport - to get to matches at their new 100,000 capacity stadium
input.summary: Fans of Manchester United are being told to use public transport - to get to matches at their new 100,000 capacity stadium
input.evidence_text: Supporters of Manchester United will be "nudged" into using public transport to travel to matches at the club's planned new 100,000 capacity stadium The club announced they want to build their new ground on the site of what is now an indoor go-karting track in Trafford Park, after they acquired the land earlier this year A strategic masterplan document, which has been launched for the Trafford Wharfside area, states: "Existing surface car parks must be consolidated and removed to make best use of available land" The leader of Trafford Council Tom Ross said "car reliance was something we need to work though" adding they needed to "nudge people towards public transport alternatives" The Trafford Wharfside Strategic Masterplan covers an area of 150 hectares, with much of the land currently dominated by surface car parks, which are used by Manchester United supporters on matchdays There are currently 11 official matchday car parks surrounding Old Trafford, with hundreds of spaces available to supporters, including those with blue badges

title: Bomb hoaxer who targeted Andy Burnham, MPs and judges sentenced

input.summary: Anthony McGrath was given a community order and told he must undergo mental health treatment

My army is much bigger considering I have Russian and Chinese support. We will be armed and shoot to kill."

title: What is the Greater Manchester mayor and what do they do?

input.summary: Andy Burnham's victory in the Makerfield by-election means he has to step down as Greater Manchester mayor Burnham has been mayor since the role was first created in 2017 and the race to become his successor will be fiercely contested by all involved But what is the role they wil
input.evidence_text: Andy Burnham's victory in the Makerfield by-election means he has to step down as Greater Manchester mayor Burnham has been mayor since the role was first created in 2017 and the race to become his successor will be fiercely contested by all involved But what is the role they will be fighting for? The role of mayor was created as part of a law passed in 2016, one of a number of big pieces of devolution legislation brought in by the Conservatives after taking power in 2010 In a 2012 referendum, Manchester rejected having a directly-elected city mayor, along with eight other major cities, but the decision to have a regional or metropolitan mayor was directly negotiated between the government and the 10 Greater Manchester councils Andy Burnham, who left Parliament to stand for the role, was chosen as Labour's candidate for the first mayoral election in 2017 and won, taking 63.4% of the vote

title: How do I register to vote in the May 2026 elections?

input.lead: Click to expand How do I register to vote?
input.summary: Click to expand How do I register to vote? Millions of voters across England, Scotland and Wales are heading to the polls on 7 May
input.evidence_text: Click to expand How do I register to vote? Millions of voters across England, Scotland and Wales are heading to the polls on 7 May. Depending where you are, you might be voting to elect your local council members, a new mayor, members of the Welsh Senedd, or Scottish Parliament But if you're going to vote, you need to make sure you are registered. The deadline for registering to vote has passed but there's still time to apply for a postal or proxy vote Here's what you need to know about how and when you register to vote Registering only takes a few minutes if you do it online. It’s the same process whether you plan to vote in person at a polling station, by post, or through a proxy voter Go to the gov.uk website and fill out the form. You'll need your National Insurance number and current address details to hand If you can't provide your NI number you can still complete the online form but you'll need to provide documents like a passport or utility bill to your local electoral registration office before you're properly registered

input.evidence_text: Supporters of Manchester United will be "nudged" into using public transport to travel to matches at the club's planned new 100,000 capacity stadium The club announced they want to build their new ground on the site of what is now an indoor go-karting track in Trafford Park, after they acquired the land earlier this year A strategic masterplan document, which has been launched for the Trafford Wharfside area, states: "Existing surface car parks must be consolidated and removed to make best use of available land" The leader of Trafford Council Tom Ross said "car reliance was something we need to work though" adding they needed to "nudge people towards public transport alternatives" The Trafford Wharfside Strategic Masterplan covers an area of 150 hectares, with much of the land currently dominated by surface car parks, which are used by Manchester United supporters on matchdays There are currently 11 official matchday car parks surrounding Old Trafford, with hundreds of spaces available to supporters, including those with blue badges

input.summary: Metrolink Snakes, heatwaves and screens - inside the rooms that keep Greater Manchester moving "I never thought I would do it. I was doing dead end office jobs before but I love doing this" View 6 Images It’s a big operation with around 1,100 people e Comments News Edward Barnes
input.evidence_text: Metrolink Snakes, heatwaves and screens - inside the rooms that keep Greater Manchester moving "I never thought I would do it. I was doing dead end office jobs before but I love doing this" View 6 Images It’s a big operation with around 1,100 people e Comments News Edward Barnes Local Democracy Reporter 07:19, 12 Jul 2026 “If you can imagine anything, it’s probably happened on the network. A few weeks ago we had a snake that somebody had brought on at the airport and it got into the roof panels “Every day there is something really different or bizarre that happens. Anything you can imagine, that has happened.” Ian Davies, Metrolink ’s network director is speaking to the Manchester Evening News within the heart of the tram network’s Trafford depot. On a typical day, the service takes 140,000 people across Greater Manchester covering over 100km and 99 tram stops It’s a big operation with around 1,100 people employed directly and, the Manchester Evening News was told, around 100 different contractors. Currently run by KeolisAmey, it is overseen by Transport for Greater Manchester The service is the most extensive light rail system in the UK and in 2024, 46 million people used the service. On a typical day, around 120 trams are out on the network but this can rise to 130 out of a total of 147 on busy days setting off at 5am and coming back at 2am

title: His passport was stolen in 2024, now he's 'trapped' in the Philippines and lives on oatmeal

input.lead: Bolton His passport was stolen in 2024, now he's 'trapped' in the Philippines and lives on oatmeal Stephen Hall, from Bolton, fears he may become destitute and never return home View 3 Images Stephen Hall shows the capsules where he is sleeping (Image News John Scheerhout Crime r
input.summary: Bolton His passport was stolen in 2024, now he's 'trapped' in the Philippines and lives on oatmeal Stephen Hall, from Bolton, fears he may become destitute and never return home View 3 Images Stephen Hall shows the capsules where he is sleeping (Image News John Scheerhout Crime r
input.evidence_text: Bolton His passport was stolen in 2024, now he's 'trapped' in the Philippines and lives on oatmeal Stephen Hall, from Bolton, fears he may become destitute and never return home View 3 Images Stephen Hall shows the capsules where he is sleeping (Image News John Scheerhout Crime reporter 07:18, 12 Jul 2026 A man from Bolton has described how he is running out of money and surviving on oatmeal and bread after becoming stranded in the Philippines 18 months ago Former record shop owner Stephen Hall, 68, embarked on a tour of Asia but his trip hit trouble almost immediately after landing in Manila, the capital city, in December 2024 He was on his way to watch Ian McNabb, the former frontman of the band Icicle Works, headline a festival in the capital city when he says two men on motorbikes snatched his bag which contained his passport, phone, camera, reading glasses, concert ticket, bank card and cash It was at that moment his dream open-ended tour of Asia which was to take in Vietnam, Malaysia and Thailand suddenly turned into a living nightmare. Well-travelled Stephen had planned to end his tour in Georgia where he intended to get work teaching English Now, after an administrative nightmare following the theft of his passport, he has almost run out of money, fears being thrown out onto the streets and has almost lost all hope of coming back home 18 months later

title: What on earth is going on with Barton Arcade?

input.lead: Manchester City Centre What on earth is going on with Barton Arcade?
input.summary: Manchester City Centre What on earth is going on with Barton Arcade? It endured the Blitz, recessions, Covid and an IRA bomb, but Barton Arcade may not survive its latest challenge View 4 Images The rusting and peeling steelwork at Barton Arcade (Imag News Damon Wilkinson Reporte
input.evidence_text: Manchester City Centre What on earth is going on with Barton Arcade? It endured the Blitz, recessions, Covid and an IRA bomb, but Barton Arcade may not survive its latest challenge View 4 Images The rusting and peeling steelwork at Barton Arcade (Imag News Damon Wilkinson Reporter 07:17, 12 Jul 2026 It has endured Covid, several recessions and being bombed during the Manchester Blitz. But campaigners fear Barton Arcade may not get through its latest challenge The 150-year-old Grade II*-listed building is the city's only surviving example of the type of steel-framed, glass-covered Victorian shopping arcade that was once popular across northern town and cities Inspired by the world-renowned Galleria Vittorio Emanuele II in Milan, it's been described as a 'jewel' in Manchester's crown. But last week the grand and elegant arcade, which links Deansgate and St Ann's Square, was one of nine Greater Manchester buildings placed on SAVE Britai Campaigners fear that without much-needed maintenance and repairs its future hangs in the balance. Of particular concern is the magnificent 53ft tall steel and glass roof, which is thought to have long been in dire need of refurbishment Remarkably it's said damage caused by the 1996 IRA bomb is still waiting to be repaired. Several glass panes are said to be cracked and broken, while from the outside the roof's steel frame is visibly rusting with paint flaking off

According to sources familiar with the discussions, Starmer urged Craig to stand as Labour's candidat The approach reflected a growing concern at the top of Labour. But the Theatre of Dreams is set to be relegated to the pages of history as the club looks to build a new stadium The proposed location for the new home ground – around 350m away from Old Trafford – was revealed this week . While it will not be built on land currently attached to the stadium, United insists it will honour the current ground’s history and traditions. Supporters have been prom At 100,000 seats, it would be the largest grounds in the United Kingdom, and will form the centrepiece of what is being called a Stadium District – an area purpose-built for sport, entertainment and year-round activity It will also be the catalyst for a huge regeneration project in Trafford which promises around 15,000 new homes, 48,000 new jobs, a possible new train station, green spaces and places for a new community to come together to eat, drink, shop and play But, for some fans, excitement for the future is tinged with sadness over the loss of what is such a huge part of the club’s history

input.evidence_text: Oldham Athletic FC Oldham Athletic pay tribute to former chairman Ken Bates following his death aged 94 Bates, best known for his time at Chelsea, was also briefly a part-owner of Wigan Athletic News Damon Wilkinson Reporter 16:48, 11 Jul 2026 View Im Oldham Athletic have paid tribute to the club's former chairman and owner Ken Bates following his death aged 94 Bates, best known for his time at Chelsea, bought the Latics in 1965 and spent five years at the helm at Boundary Park . He later spent a short time as part-owner of owner of Wigan Athletic before he famously bought Chelsea, then in financial dire straits, for £1 in 1982 In a statement today Oldham Athletic said: "We are saddened to learn of the passing of our former chairman, Ken Bates, aged 94. We send our thoughts and condolences to Ken's family and friends at this time." Bates spent 22 years at Stamford Bridge, establishing the club towards the top end of the Premier League before selling up to Roman Abramovich for £140m in 2003. He then took over at Leeds in 2005, where he also served as chairman Chelsea said Bates died peacefully on Saturday morning surrounded by his wife and family in Monaco

Ofsted’s ‘new look’ report cards have moved away from one-word summaries like ‘good’, ‘outstanding’, or ‘requires improvement’ Instead inspectors give a descriptive assessment aimed at guardians that ‘gives a clearer picture of what’s working well and where the next steps for improvement are’. The report cards show colour-coded grades for different areas like ‘attendance and behaviour’, ‘achievement’, ‘p These areas on a new scale of ‘exceptional’, ‘strong standard’, ‘expected standard’, ‘needs attention’ and ‘urgent improvement’ Ofsted also states whether safeguarding responsibilities are ‘met’ or ‘not met’ Below is a summary of every Ofsted inspection published for Greater Manchester primary and secondary schools for the period June 22 to July 9. But what followed was a journey that saw her at deaths door before she found refuge in Manchester Aaisha, who's name has been changed to protect her identity, was still just a teenager when fighting erupted in Bosnia in May 1992. The outbreak lead to years of intense fighting between Bosnian Serbs, Bosnian Muslims and Bosnian Croats who all once stood united under the banner The bloody conflict led to the displacement of Bosnian Muslims, known as Bosniaks, who were systematically imprisoned, tortured, starved, and murdered by Serbian paramilitary troops The war culminated in the largest mass murder on European soil since the World War Two, when over 8,000 Bosnian Muslim men and boys were captured and executed in the town of Srebrenica in July 1995

I was doing dead end office jobs before but I love doing this" View 6 Images It’s a big operation with around 1,100 people e Comments News Edward Barnes Local Democracy Reporter 07:19, 12 Jul 2026 “If you can imagine anything, it’s probably happened on the network. A few weeks ago we had a snake that somebody had brought on at the airport and it got into the roof panels “Every day there is something really different or bizarre that happens. Anything you can imagine, that has happened.” Ian Davies, Metrolink ’s network director is speaking to the Manchester Evening News within the heart of the tram network’s Trafford depot. On a typical day, the service takes 140,000 people across Greater Manchester covering over 100km and 99 tram stops It’s a big operation with around 1,100 people employed directly and, the Manchester Evening News was told, around 100 different contractors. Currently run by KeolisAmey, it is overseen by Transport for Greater Manchester The service is the most extensive light rail system in the UK and in 2024, 46 million people used the service. On a typical day, around 120 trams are out on the network but this can rise to 130 out of a total of 147 on busy days setting off at 5am and coming back at 2am

input.evidence_text: Bolton His passport was stolen in 2024, now he's 'trapped' in the Philippines and lives on oatmeal Stephen Hall, from Bolton, fears he may become destitute and never return home View 3 Images Stephen Hall shows the capsules where he is sleeping (Image News John Scheerhout Crime reporter 07:18, 12 Jul 2026 A man from Bolton has described how he is running out of money and surviving on oatmeal and bread after becoming stranded in the Philippines 18 months ago Former record shop owner Stephen Hall, 68, embarked on a tour of Asia but his trip hit trouble almost immediately after landing in Manila, the capital city, in December 2024 He was on his way to watch Ian McNabb, the former frontman of the band Icicle Works, headline a festival in the capital city when he says two men on motorbikes snatched his bag which contained his passport, phone, camera, reading glasses, concert ticket, bank card and cash It was at that moment his dream open-ended tour of Asia which was to take in Vietnam, Malaysia and Thailand suddenly turned into a living nightmare. Well-travelled Stephen had planned to end his tour in Georgia where he intended to get work teaching English Now, after an administrative nightmare following the theft of his passport, he has almost run out of money, fears being thrown out onto the streets and has almost lost all hope of coming back home 18 months later

title: What on earth is going on with Barton Arcade?

input.lead: Manchester City Centre What on earth is going on with Barton Arcade?
input.summary: Manchester City Centre What on earth is going on with Barton Arcade? It endured the Blitz, recessions, Covid and an IRA bomb, but Barton Arcade may not survive its latest challenge View 4 Images The rusting and peeling steelwork at Barton Arcade (Imag News Damon Wilkinson Reporte
input.evidence_text: Manchester City Centre What on earth is going on with Barton Arcade? It endured the Blitz, recessions, Covid and an IRA bomb, but Barton Arcade may not survive its latest challenge View 4 Images The rusting and peeling steelwork at Barton Arcade (Imag News Damon Wilkinson Reporter 07:17, 12 Jul 2026 It has endured Covid, several recessions and being bombed during the Manchester Blitz. But campaigners fear Barton Arcade may not get through its latest challenge The 150-year-old Grade II*-listed building is the city's only surviving example of the type of steel-framed, glass-covered Victorian shopping arcade that was once popular across northern town and cities Inspired by the world-renowned Galleria Vittorio Emanuele II in Milan, it's been described as a 'jewel' in Manchester's crown. But last week the grand and elegant arcade, which links Deansgate and St Ann's Square, was one of nine Greater Manchester buildings placed on SAVE Britai Campaigners fear that without much-needed maintenance and repairs its future hangs in the balance. Of particular concern is the magnificent 53ft tall steel and glass roof, which is thought to have long been in dire need of refurbishment Remarkably it's said damage caused by the 1996 IRA bomb is still waiting to be repaired. Several glass panes are said to be cracked and broken, while from the outside the roof's steel frame is visibly rusting with paint flaking off

input.evidence_text: Greater Manchester mayoral election 2026 Keir Starmer urged Bev Craig to stand in Gorton and Denton by-election after Andy Burnham was blocked EXCLUSIVE: The Manchester Evening News can reveal that, after Andy Burnham was blocked from standing in Gort News Iram Ramzan 07:17, 12 Jul 2026 For a brief moment, Keir Starmer thought he had found a canny way to justify one of the most contentious Labour candidate decisions in recent memory The Prime Minister personally approached Bev Craig, the leader of Manchester City Council, after Andy Burnham had been blocked from standing in the Gorton and Denton by-election. According to sources familiar with the discussions, Starmer urged Craig to stand as Labour's candidat The approach reflected a growing concern at the top of Labour. While it will not be built on land currently attached to the stadium, United insists it will honour the current ground’s history and traditions. Supporters have been prom At 100,000 seats, it would be the largest grounds in the United Kingdom, and will form the centrepiece of what is being called a Stadium District – an area purpose-built for sport, entertainment and year-round activity It will also be the catalyst for a huge regeneration project in Trafford which promises around 15,000 new homes, 48,000 new jobs, a possible new train station, green spaces and places for a new community to come together to eat, drink, shop and play But, for some fans, excitement for the future is tinged with sadness over the loss of what is such a huge part of the club’s history

Decades later, the site was converted into an auxiliary hospital by the British Red Cross and cared for wounded soldiers during the First Worl While it closed at the end of the war, an increasingly apparent need for a hospital in the local area saw it reopen as the Stretford Memorial Hospital in 1925 It became part of the NHS in 1948 and, save for a brief period of closure in the 1980s, treated patients until its doors shut for good in 2015. Anything you can imagine, that has happened.” Ian Davies, Metrolink ’s network director is speaking to the Manchester Evening News within the heart of the tram network’s Trafford depot. On a typical day, the service takes 140,000 people across Greater Manchester covering over 100km and 99 tram stops It’s a big operation with around 1,100 people employed directly and, the Manchester Evening News was told, around 100 different contractors. Currently run by KeolisAmey, it is overseen by Transport for Greater Manchester The service is the most extensive light rail system in the UK and in 2024, 46 million people used the service. title: What on earth is going on with Barton Arcade?

input.lead: Manchester City Centre What on earth is going on with Barton Arcade?
input.summary: Manchester City Centre What on earth is going on with Barton Arcade? It endured the Blitz, recessions, Covid and an IRA bomb, but Barton Arcade may not survive its latest challenge View 4 Images The rusting and peeling steelwork at Barton Arcade (Imag News Damon Wilkinson Reporte
input.evidence_text: Manchester City Centre What on earth is going on with Barton Arcade? It endured the Blitz, recessions, Covid and an IRA bomb, but Barton Arcade may not survive its latest challenge View 4 Images The rusting and peeling steelwork at Barton Arcade (Imag News Damon Wilkinson Reporter 07:17, 12 Jul 2026 It has endured Covid, several recessions and being bombed during the Manchester Blitz. But campaigners fear Barton Arcade may not get through its latest challenge The 150-year-old Grade II*-listed building is the city's only surviving example of the type of steel-framed, glass-covered Victorian shopping arcade that was once popular across northern town and cities Inspired by the world-renowned Galleria Vittorio Emanuele II in Milan, it's been described as a 'jewel' in Manchester's crown. But last week the grand and elegant arcade, which links Deansgate and St Ann's Square, was one of nine Greater Manchester buildings placed on SAVE Britai Campaigners fear that without much-needed maintenance and repairs its future hangs in the balance. Of particular concern is the magnificent 53ft tall steel and glass roof, which is thought to have long been in dire need of refurbishment Remarkably it's said damage caused by the 1996 IRA bomb is still waiting to be repaired. Several glass panes are said to be cracked and broken, while from the outside the roof's steel frame is visibly rusting with paint flaking off

input.evidence_text: Manchester United FC 'This is Manchester United's home, it's a shame it won't be any more' Fans of the Red Devils react to huge plans for a new 100,000-seater stadium, around 350m away from Old Trafford Comments News Hannah Richardson 17:24, 11 Jul 20 Old Trafford has been home to Manchester United since 1910 and the stadium is legendary among football fans across the world. But the Theatre of Dreams is set to be relegated to the pages of history as the club looks to build a new stadium The proposed location for the new home ground – around 350m away from Old Trafford – was revealed this week . While it will not be built on land currently attached to the stadium, United insists it will honour the current ground’s history and traditions. Supporters have been prom At 100,000 seats, it would be the largest grounds in the United Kingdom, and will form the centrepiece of what is being called a Stadium District – an area purpose-built for sport, entertainment and year-round activity It will also be the catalyst for a huge regeneration project in Trafford which promises around 15,000 new homes, 48,000 new jobs, a possible new train station, green spaces and places for a new community to come together to eat, drink, shop and play But, for some fans, excitement for the future is tinged with sadness over the loss of what is such a huge part of the club’s history

He later spent a short time as part-owner of owner of Wigan Athletic before he famously bought Chelsea, then in financial dire straits, for £1 in 1982 In a statement today Oldham Athletic said: "We are saddened to learn of the passing of our former chairman, Ken Bates, aged 94. We send our thoughts and condolences to Ken's family and friends at this time." Bates spent 22 years at Stamford Bridge, establishing the club towards the top end of the Premier League before selling up to Roman Abramovich for £140m in 2003. He then took over at Leeds in 2005, where he also served as chairman Chelsea said Bates died peacefully on Saturday morning surrounded by his wife and family in Monaco

Ofsted’s ‘new look’ report cards have moved away from one-word summaries like ‘good’, ‘outstanding’, or ‘requires improvement’ Instead inspectors give a descriptive assessment aimed at guardians that ‘gives a clearer picture of what’s working well and where the next steps for improvement are’. The report cards show colour-coded grades for different areas like ‘attendance and behaviour’, ‘achievement’, ‘p These areas on a new scale of ‘exceptional’, ‘strong standard’, ‘expected standard’, ‘needs attention’ and ‘urgent improvement’ Ofsted also states whether safeguarding responsibilities are ‘met’ or ‘not met’ Below is a summary of every Ofsted inspection published for Greater Manchester primary and secondary schools for the period June 22 to July 9. But what followed was a journey that saw her at deaths door before she found refuge in Manchester Aaisha, who's name has been changed to protect her identity, was still just a teenager when fighting erupted in Bosnia in May 1992. The outbreak lead to years of intense fighting between Bosnian Serbs, Bosnian Muslims and Bosnian Croats who all once stood united under the banner The bloody conflict led to the displacement of Bosnian Muslims, known as Bosniaks, who were systematically imprisoned, tortured, starved, and murdered by Serbian paramilitary troops The war culminated in the largest mass murder on European soil since the World War Two, when over 8,000 Bosnian Muslim men and boys were captured and executed in the town of Srebrenica in July 1995

Decades later, the site was converted into an auxiliary hospital by the British Red Cross and cared for wounded soldiers during the First Worl While it closed at the end of the war, an increasingly apparent need for a hospital in the local area saw it reopen as the Stretford Memorial Hospital in 1925 It became part of the NHS in 1948 and, save for a brief period of closure in the 1980s, treated patients until its doors shut for good in 2015. No. Andy Burnham cannot be mayor and a member of parliament. He had seven days - from the moment of his election - to resign from the post which he has held for almost a decade He handed in his resignation on 19 June - triggering the process of the by-election Burnham cannot do both jobs because the Mayor of Greater Manchester holds Police and Crime Commissioner (PCC) powers and UK law strictly dictates that the office holder cannot simultaneously serve as an MP

title: The bizarre story behind the ‘Salford University rape cover up' posters

input.evidence_text: For months an anonymous figure has been levelling accusations at the university. We finally spoke to them A student walks into Salford University’s support office and douses themselves in petrol. “Not as theatre,” they would later write, “but as a last available language” Now they recount the story over the phone. As they explain it, they’d entered the room “presenting female” and carrying a large green Jerry can, sat down in a chair before a member of staff and said: “Do you think I look gay? Do you think I look gay now?” When security tried to restrain them, the student poured the contents of the Jerry can over themselves and took out a lighter. They can’t recall now if they threatened to set themselves on fire, but they do remember flicking the small round striker with their thumb This is the story behind the posters that have been plastered remarkably far and wide across Greater Manchester: in Salford, Bolton, Wigan, Stockport, as far as Oldham, as close as Deansgate. The text in them varies, but they all make the same accusation: that Salford University A journalist posing with one of the posters on Mosley Street reached 50,000 viewers on Instagram, asking them if they’d send their children to a university “if you knew that they’d covered up rape”. title: A dish best served cold: A TV historian sued us to reveal our sources - and lost

input.summary: Laurence Westgaph waged a legal campaign to find out who had spoken to one of our reporters. A year later, we met him in court
input.evidence_text: Laurence Westgaph waged a legal campaign to find out who had spoken to one of our reporters. A year later, we met him in court On the first day of the trial earlier this month, TV historian Laurence Westgaph was asked to turn to page 255 of the hearing bundle: the thick ringbinder of documents that chronicle a year-long legal battle that was now, finally, coming to a head in court Westgaph, a well-known figure in Liverpool who has presented history shows on the BBC, was asked to read a Facebook post he’d published about me in July last year. He began to read it out “I served the big busta, Mr Herrmann this week…” Our barrister, Claire Overman, stopped him. She hadn’t meant for him to read the post out loud. Everyone in court read the Facebook post in silence. “This is just the starter though,” Westgaph had written on top of an image of himself staring at the camera with his shirt unbutton This was the denouement of a tough hour-long cross-examination of Westgaph, in which it often looked like he was struggling to explain why he had initiated this lawsuit in the first place. Claire, representing Mill Media, asked him about the dish “best served cold”

title: Let's get metaphysical: could an ex-philosopher be the new Burnham?

input.lead: She was one of Greater Manchester's first Green councillors.
input.summary: She was one of Greater Manchester's first Green councillors. Now she has her eyes on a bigger prize
input.evidence_text: She was one of Greater Manchester's first Green councillors. Now she has her eyes on a bigger prize Before politics, one interest dominated the life of Geraldine Coggins, leader of the Trafford Greens, candidate for Greater Manchester mayor, and that was metaphysical nihilism. The now-politician harbours a lifelong love of philosophy, studying the subject up and down the UK and What is metaphysical nihilism? When I ask her, Coggins seems reluctant to discuss it, directing me instead towards the book’s synopsis. “The content is very abstract,” she tells me, “and doesn’t affect my worldview now.” Maybe Coggins is trying to draw a distinction between the r And what a time in the Green Party it’s been. When I meet her, drinking matcha outside a glass-fronted MediaCity cafe, she runs me through the last decade-and-a-half in her Irish lilt: a councillor for eight years, a group leader for four; becoming one of Trafford's first Green c Coggins possesses a demeanour at once warm and authoritative, and it’s this unlikely mix that I’m told helped her win the favour of Altrincham’s electorate in 2018, when, alongside councillor Daniel Jerrome, she made history Two seats were vacant in the ward that year, and the pair stood as the Green candidates. Jerrome recalls a febrile atmosphere on polling day, with neither opposition parties nor the press able to predict what was to come

title: Chezdan was young, gay and living in a council house in Bolton. He also wanted to be on TV — at any cost

input.lead: Aged ten and eight, my brother and I appeared on the Saturday morning horror-comedy show Ross Lee’s Ghoulies .
input.summary: Aged ten and eight, my brother and I appeared on the Saturday morning horror-comedy show Ross Lee’s Ghoulies . Our task was this: we were to dress as zombies and crouch in the bushes at the local golf course while our Dad enjoyed a morning of hooks and slices
input.evidence_text: Aged ten and eight, my brother and I appeared on the Saturday morning horror-comedy show Ross Lee’s Ghoulies . Our task was this: we were to dress as zombies and crouch in the bushes at the local golf course while our Dad enjoyed a morning of hooks and slices. When he reached the There followed the ghoulies, four of them, a Nickelodeon-troupe of pasty white zombies waddling out from the shrubbery, followed by serial prankster and TV host Ross Lee (who would eventually hit dizzying heights with the Keith Lemon Sketch Show). When we got to the studio in Lon Chezdan Mills had his own post-TV fame realisation that same year, 2009. His came at a bus stop The show Chezdan had been on had a viewership more than a touch higher than Ross Lee’s Ghoulies . At least, while I don’t think Chezdan can recall seeing me and my brother standing proud before the Scareometer, I distinctly recall watching him, arguing with an irate Pastor on BBC At that time, British reality TV operated under a defining principle. The key was to be as nasty as possible to ordinary people, and to make them appear as ludicrous as possible for the entertainment of the viewing public. Bonus points could be earned if they were poor, or 16 Chezdan was 16. He came from Bolton. He wanted — at just about any cost — to be on the TV

input.evidence_text: ‘Your future has no limits’ is the inspiring message being shared with young people with additional needs and disabilities across the borough of Rochdale This follows a careers event organised by Rochdale’s additional needs service (RANS), part of Rochdale Borough Council, at Middleton Arena to inspire the career aspirations of local pupils aged 10-14 with special educational needs and disabilities (SEND) During a day packed full of careers advice, more than 60 pupils from nearby schools had the opportunity to hear from a number of successful professionals with additional needs This included 2-time Paralympic gold medallist and double amputee runner, Richard Whitehead MBE, who also holds the world record for the fastest marathon by a leg amputee This event came as national figures highlight only half the number of people in the UK with a disability aged 16-64 are in employment (52.8%), compared to 4 out of 5 people (82.5%) who don’t have a disability Local services have been working hard however, to address this challenge by highlighting the successful careers and futures that young people can achieve with a disability as they grow up

title: More than a game: football's role in one family's fostering journey - Rochdale Borough Council

input.lead: A foster family from Heywood have opened up about how their love for football has helped to create the ultimate loving environment for the children in their care
input.summary: A foster family from Heywood have opened up about how their love for football has helped to create the ultimate loving environment for the children in their care
input.evidence_text: A foster family from Heywood have opened up about how their love for football has helped to create the ultimate loving environment for the children in their care Lauren (46) and her husband Danny Sullivan (52) have fostered for Rochdale Borough Council since 2012 and currently care for 5 young lads aged 12 to 21, alongside their 9-year-old son, Daniel Their home is very busy as you can imagine and ‘the washing machine is non-stop’ Lauren jokes. Each child has their own interests, including things like the gym and gaming, but football is what stands out as the cement in their daily routines From training and coaching on weekdays to refereeing and playing matches at the weekend, the house is football mad and it’s their passion for the sport which connects them as a family and what has helped the lads to create a strong brotherly bond “They’ll all come home after school, college or work, and Daniel will ask them for a game,” Lauren said, with a pair of football nets visible outside from the living room “It’s so lovely to see the smiles on their faces when they’re all playing together in the back garden.”

title: New funding will help the council tackle the sticky issue of chewing gum on Rochdale's streets - Rochdale Borough Council

input.lead: Rochdale Borough Council has been awarded a grant from the Chewing Gum Task Force, administered by environmental charity Keep Britain Tidy, to help clean up gum and reduce gum littering
input.summary: Rochdale Borough Council has been awarded a grant from the Chewing Gum Task Force, administered by environmental charity Keep Britain Tidy, to help clean up gum and reduce gum littering
input.evidence_text: Rochdale Borough Council has been awarded a grant from the Chewing Gum Task Force, administered by environmental charity Keep Britain Tidy, to help clean up gum and reduce gum littering Today, the council announced plans to step up the removal of chewing gum that blights Rochdale town centre after receiving a £27,461 grant to tackle the issue Rochdale is one of 50 councils across the country that have successfully applied to the Chewing Gum Task Force, now in its fifth year, for funds to clean gum off pavements and prevent it from being littered in the first place The Chewing Gum Task Force grant scheme – established by Defra (Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs) and run by environmental charity Keep Britain Tidy – is open to councils across the UK who wish to clean up gum in their local areas and invest in long-term behavio The Task Force is funded by major gum manufacturers including Mars Wrigley and Perfetti Van Melle, with an investment of up to £10 million spread over 5 years In the past 4 years, the Task Force has awarded grants worth a total of £6.46 million, funded the cleaning of over 4.15 million square metres of pavements

title: Encouraging decline in youth smoking and vaping across Wigan Borough

input.lead: Wigan Borough is seeing a positive decline in the number of young people smoking and regularly vaping, according to a recent Trading Standards survey Following national concerns about youth smoking and vaping, Wigan Council has made it a priority to reduce these figures locally a
input.summary: Wigan Borough is seeing a positive decline in the number of young people smoking and regularly vaping, according to a recent Trading Standards survey Following national concerns about youth smoking and vaping, Wigan Council has made it a priority to reduce these figures locally a
input.evidence_text: Wigan Borough is seeing a positive decline in the number of young people smoking and regularly vaping, according to a recent Trading Standards survey Following national concerns about youth smoking and vaping, Wigan Council has made it a priority to reduce these figures locally and protect the health of our children and young people With more than 1,000 school children aged 14-17 included in the Trading Standards survey, the findings show the number of young people who have tried vaping dropped from around 44 per cent in 2023 to 18 per cent in 2026 The survey, carried out between November 2025 and February 2026, also highlights that smoking is at an all-time low, with around 97 per cent reporting to have never tried smoking Councillor Keith Cunliffe, Deputy Leader of Wigan Council and cabinet portfolio holder for adult social care and health, said: “I’m really pleased to see smoking and vaping rates among young people in Wigan Borough continuing to fall “Reducing tobacco and nicotine use among young people is one of the most effective ways to prevent long-term ill health and reduce health inequalities in our communities

title: Marina Abramović: Balkan Erotic Epic | Aviva Studios

input.lead: Experience the raw, unfiltered power of Balkan folklore in a new immersive show from the world’s most famous performance artist Balkan Erotic Epic is a bold new performance work created by the iconic artist Marina Abramović .
input.summary: Experience the raw, unfiltered power of Balkan folklore in a new immersive show from the world’s most famous performance artist Balkan Erotic Epic is a bold new performance work created by the iconic artist Marina Abramović . Regarded as the world’s most influential performance a
input.evidence_text: Experience the raw, unfiltered power of Balkan folklore in a new immersive show from the world’s most famous performance artist Balkan Erotic Epic is a bold new performance work created by the iconic artist Marina Abramović . Regarded as the world’s most influential performance artist, Abramović’s transformative work has reached millions of people – from opening the Pyramid Stage at Glastonbury Festival t A four-hour ritual where ancient myth meets performance art, Balkan Erotic Epic explores the eroticism, spirituality and traditions of Abramović’s homeland through 13 visceral scenes Brought to life by a cast of over 70 performers, dancers, musicians and singers, the experience unfolds across Aviva Studios. Audiences are free to choose their own path – with pop-up encounters punctuating the action, bringing bodies together in dance, song and ritual Watch Fertility Rite , a fevered ritual where bodies writhe against the ground in a desperate call for fertility. Witness Massaging the Breast where women gesticulate over graves to awaken the earth. In Scaring the Gods , women bare themselves to the sky to banish storms. Listen Unafraid to push the boundaries of eroticism and performance art, Balkan Erotic Epic asks its audience a timeless question: What are our bodies truly for?

Join Manchester Vocal Ensemble, one of the city’s most exciting vocal groups, for an electrifying afternoon where iconic songs are transformed through spine-tingling vocal harmony. From Adele and Fleetwood Mac to Hozier and Queen, hear legendary artists reimagined through rich, layered harmonies and a beautifully blended sound. Each performance is crafted to captivate, uplift and surprise — turning familiar hits into something thrillingly new. This collective of outstanding singers brings a bold creative vision to contemporary music, performing completely original arrangements that reveal new colours, textures and emotion in the songs you love. Expect powerful anthems, breathtaking ballads and unforgettable musical moments as we take you on a journey from chart-topping pop to the magic of film and theatre. Whether you love great pop music, stunning vocal harmony, or simply a brilliant live night out, this is a show not to miss. title: Banner Bingo x International Mother Language Day - People's History Museum

input.lead: For International Mother Language Day 2026 People’s History Museum launched Banner Bingo in an additional seven languages Alongside English, visitors can take their pick from Arabic, Chinese, French, Persian, Polish, Spanish, and Urdu as they take part in the free Family Friendly
input.summary: For International Mother Language Day 2026 People’s History Museum launched Banner Bingo in an additional seven languages Alongside English, visitors can take their pick from Arabic, Chinese, French, Persian, Polish, Spanish, and Urdu as they take part in the free Family Friendly
input.evidence_text: For International Mother Language Day 2026 People’s History Museum launched Banner Bingo in an additional seven languages Alongside English, visitors can take their pick from Arabic, Chinese, French, Persian, Polish, Spanish, and Urdu as they take part in the free Family Friendly activity at the museum Banner Bingo is a quest to find a series of images featured within the colourful banners that are on display throughout the museum’s galleries. You can pick up a Banner Bingo card from the welcome desk, and after completing your search there is a small prize for taking part. Two Part of a programme of activities organised by Manchester City of Literature to celebrate International Mother Language Day The translations of Banner Bingo were provided by Peak Translations Ltd, thebigword, The Translation People and the Association of Translation Companies Are you more independent or community focused? title: The Fabric of Protest, Sat 18 July 2026, 1.00pm - 3.00pm - People's History Museum

input.lead: Join this creative workshop for some stitching, chatting, and sharing inspired by the museum’s collections What is the right to strike and is it under threat?
input.summary: Join this creative workshop for some stitching, chatting, and sharing inspired by the museum’s collections What is the right to strike and is it under threat? In this workshop we will visit the On The Line: 100 years of strikes & solidarity exhibition to explore how workers first
input.evidence_text: Join this creative workshop for some stitching, chatting, and sharing inspired by the museum’s collections What is the right to strike and is it under threat? In this workshop we will visit the On The Line: 100 years of strikes & solidarity exhibition to explore how workers first secured the right to strike in the 1800s, how strike protections have changed and how that has impacted us Participants will create textile responses to the discussions, which will be used as part of a collective textile piece in future workshops Part of PHM’s programme of activity exploring the history of strikes and solidarity Suitable for 12+ (under 16s must have an accompanying adult) If you require British Sign Language (BSL) interpretation please contact access@phm.org.uk or 0161 838 9190

title: Young Musician's Summer School

input.lead: We’re inviting young people to explore music for film and compose their own soundtrack this summer!
input.summary: We’re inviting young people to explore music for film and compose their own soundtrack this summer! […] The post Young Musician’s Summer School appeared first on Band on the Wall
input.evidence_text: Young Musician’s Summer School . Young Musician’s Summer School. . Book here . . . . . . . Friday | 03.07.2026 . . . World of Music . Share . . . . --> . . We’re inviting young people to explore music for film and compose their own soundtrack this summer! Whether your child already attends our Saturday Music Club , or just wants to try something new, our Young Musician’s Summer School is suitable for all abilities! . . . . . Across this 5 day course, we’ll be exploring music for film, and working on composing music for the silent film ‘A Trip to the Moon’. The course will conclude with a live performance soundtracking the film for family and friends. . . . . We welcome all abilities and instruments to take part in this course. You can bring your own instruments, but we have keyboards, guitars, bass, violins, drums and percussion on site which can be used if you don’t have an instrument.. . . Snacks and drinks provided, and pizzas on the final day. Please bring your own packed lunch to sessions Monday-Thursday.. . Dates: Monday 17th – Friday 21st August. Times: 10:00-16:30 Monday-Thursday, then a performance for friends and family on Friday at 13:00 (students will be expected to come in at 10:00 sharp on the Friday).. Ages: 12-18 years old. Cost: £150 for the full course. Bursaries are available for those on lower income – please email learning@bandonthewall.org to arrange this.. . Spaces are limited – Book here . . . Please email learning@bandonthewall.org if you have any questions about this course

input.summary: The Only King is acclaimed as the first original Cantonese biblical musical to present the story of the Book of 1 Samuel in its entirety. The production follows the life of King Saul, from his anointing and rise to the throne to his gradual downfall through pride and disobedience
input.evidence_text: The Only King is acclaimed as the first original Cantonese biblical musical to present the story of the Book of 1 Samuel in its entirety. The production follows the life of King Saul, from his anointing and rise to the throne to his gradual downfall through pride and disobedience First staged in 2005, The Only King enjoyed multiple sold-out performances in Hong Kong and is widely regarded as a landmark work in Chinese Christian musical theatre To mark the 20th anniversary of its premiere, The Only King was presented in London and Cambridge in January 2026, where it received enthusiastic responses and strong audience acclaim. Building on this success, the production will embark on a UK tour in September 2026, with perfo Performed in Cantonese with English subtitles Suitable for audiences aged 3 and above. Children under the age of 12 must be accompanied by an adult * These tickets include an exclusive souvenir

title: Спектакль "Скамейка"

input.lead: Друзья, а вы давно ощущали настоящую магию театра?
input.summary: Друзья, а вы давно ощущали настоящую магию театра? 29 ноября 2026 года в Logan Hall — легендарная «Скамейка» с Алексеем Паниным
input.evidence_text: Спектакль «Скамейка» в Лондоне | Алексей Панин | 29 ноября 2026 :: EventFirst Home double_arrow Events double_arrow Спектакль "Скамейка" On sale Спектакль "Скамейка" schedule 29 Nov 2026 Sunday, 19:00 location_on Logan Hall 20 Bedford Way, London WC1H 0AL balcony 6:00 pm gpp_maybe NO REFUNDS Buy On MAP Buy Tickets Logan Hall £30.00 Select Logan Hall £35.00 Select Logan Hall £40.00 Select Logan Hall £45.00 Select Logan Hall £50.00 Select Logan Hall £55.00 Select Logan Hall £60.00 Select Logan Hall £65.00 Sold Out Logan Hall £70.00 Select Logan Hall £80.00 Select Logan Hall £85.00 Sold Out Logan Hall £90.00 Select Друзья, а вы давно ощущали настоящую магию театра? 29 ноября 2026 года в Logan Hall — легендарная «Скамейка» с Алексеем Паниным. После аншлагов в Европе и США спектакль впервые приезжает в Лондон. 📍 University College London, Level 1, 20 Bedford Way, London WC1H 0AL 🕕 DOORS: 18:00 🕖 SHOW: 19:00 «Скамейка» — знаменитая пьеса, когда-то блистательно сыгранная Олегом Ефремовым и Татьяной Дорониной на сцене МХАТа, а затем покорившая театральные площадки по всему миру. Это история о мужчине и женщине. О случайной встрече, которая постепенно превращается в откровенный разговор — честный, смешной, ранимый и очень человеческий. О том моменте, когда между людьми исчезают роли и, наконец, становится невозможно притворяться. На сцене — Алексей Панин и Ольга Силина. Тонкая актерская игра, живая энергия и удивительно узнаваемая история, в которой каждый услышит что-то своё. “Скамейка” — спектакль, после которого хочется немного помолчать и еще долго думать о главном. Если вам не хватает настоящего театра — этот вечер нельзя пропустить. Количество билетов ограничено. Продолжительность спектакля — 2 часа с антрактом. settings Loading venue plan... shopping_cart Your tickets: 0

Named after a combination of Oslo and Manchester, the restaurant blended Stoermann-Naess’ Norwegian heritage with Heron’s classical culinary trainin The return will see Heron resume her role as Executive Chef, with a menu centred around seasonal produce, fresh seafood, local meats and vegetable-led dishes. title: Manchester Town Hall stained glass transformed into a charity jewellery collection

input.lead: Stained glass salvaged from Manchester Town Hall has been transformed into a unique jewellery collection The Town Hall Jewellery collection has been created by Lin Fletcher, a stained-glass artist and one of the expert team tasked with repairing and restoring more than 4,000 wind
input.summary: Stained glass salvaged from Manchester Town Hall has been transformed into a unique jewellery collection The Town Hall Jewellery collection has been created by Lin Fletcher, a stained-glass artist and one of the expert team tasked with repairing and restoring more than 4,000 wind
input.evidence_text: Stained glass salvaged from Manchester Town Hall has been transformed into a unique jewellery collection The Town Hall Jewellery collection has been created by Lin Fletcher, a stained-glass artist and one of the expert team tasked with repairing and restoring more than 4,000 windows as part of the Manchester Town Hall restoration project The jewellery is now for sale at the Manchester Central Library shop with all the profits going to the Lord Mayor’s Charity. Each item in the collection, which includes earrings, pendants, and cufflinks, is a one-of-a-kind creation, complete with flaws and imperfections in the gl As part of the Town Hall renovation, windows were removed, and their condition was assessed. Much of the original glass was still in good condition; this was cleaned and reset with new leading Where it couldn’t be reused, the weakened or damaged pieces of original glass were replaced with new sections. This authentic replacement glas s (offcuts from the new glass that was brought in to replace what couldn’t be reused) was cut to shape as required and added to complete Lin recognised that these small offcuts of original and authentic glass, although unusable in the windows, had wonderful heritage value, and, as a jeweller, saw the opportunity to transform the fragments into something new

title: It Walks Around The House At Night

input.summary: A ghost story by Tim Foley · Directed by Neil Bettles Following a sell-out UK tour and London run, the award-winning ThickSkin present this chilling new play by Tim Foley (former Bruntwood Prize-winner) When an out-of-work actor takes a job playing a ghost at an old countryside m
input.evidence_text: A ghost story by Tim Foley · Directed by Neil Bettles Following a sell-out UK tour and London run, the award-winning ThickSkin present this chilling new play by Tim Foley (former Bruntwood Prize-winner) When an out-of-work actor takes a job playing a ghost at an old countryside manor, he expects nothing more than a few cheap scares. But as night after night unfolds, the role begins to blur with reality — and something far more sinister starts to emerge Part ghost story, part psychological thriller, It Walks Around The House At Night draws audiences into a world of creeping dread, unexpected twists and dark humour. A haunted house story for a new era, brought to life through inventive staging, immersive sound and bold visual eff ThickSkin, one of the UK’s most exciting touring companies, joins forces with Tim Foley (award-winning writer of Doctor Who audio dramas; Electric Rosary, Royal Exchange Theatre) to create this thrilling production. Directed by Neil Bettles (ThickSkin’s How Not To Drown, Disney’s Unsettling, darkly comic and full of surprises, the stage is set… for terror! Age Recommendation: 14+ Content Warnings: Loud and sudden noises, haze, strobe, flashing lights, occasional strong language, horror imagery and jump scares. Runtime: 90 minutes (no interval)

title: World Cup: England skipper Kane hails Haaland

input.lead: World Cup: England skipper Kane hails Haaland
input.summary: That’s according to England talisman Harry Kane with the striking superstars set to face off tonight in a thrilling World Cup quarter-final. Haaland will lead the line for Norway with Kane set to skipper England in the 22:00 (UK) knockout clash at Miami Stadium
input.evidence_text: That’s according to England talisman Harry Kane with the striking superstars set to face off tonight in a thrilling World Cup quarter-final. Haaland will lead the line for Norway with Kane set to skipper England in the 22:00 (UK) knockout clash at Miami Stadium. It’s a battle between two of the world’s most prodigious frontmen with not only progress to the last four up for grabs but also the competition’s Golden Boot in the balance with Kane on six and Haaland on seven behind only Argentina’s Lionel Messi and France’s Kylian Mbappe on eight in the fight for the prestigious individual gong. Heading into this evening’s hotly-anticipated encounter, Kane waxed lyrical about his opposite number. “Erling has been incredible,” said Kane. “Look at his goal scoring record. And physically, he’s a machine, he’s a beast. “His finishing is at another level and his goalscoring record speaks for itself. “I respect him a lot as a player and as a fellow professional. “I’m obviously hoping he has a quiet day. His performances speak for themselves. He’s a fantastic player.” The performances of both prolific forwards have led to comparisons and naturally questions of which player is better. It’s a discussion Kane refused to get drawn into primarily because he believes they’re different type of strikers. “It’s an impossible one for me to answer’” he added. “I think we are completely different players. I know we are both seen as strikers but we are almost two different positions if I’m totally honest.” City Magazine: World Cup special available now! Featuring Rayan Cherki, Erling Haaland, Omar Marmoush and a greatest ever World Cup Manchester Best XI - get reading now!

title: Man United transfer news: Ederson deal 'off' claims, Tchouameni latest, Joao Gomes rumour

input.lead: Manchester United have work to do in the transfer market in the coming weeks as Michael Carrick hopes to strengthen his side ahead of their return to the Champions League
input.summary: Manchester United have work to do in the transfer market in the coming weeks as Michael Carrick hopes to strengthen his side ahead of their return to the Champions League
input.evidence_text: Football Manchester United Transfer News LIVE Updated 57 mins ago Man United transfer news: Ederson deal 'off' claims, Tchouameni latest, Joao Gomes rumour Manchester United have work to do in the transfer market in the coming weeks as Michael Carrick hopes to strengthen hi United have been proactive to kickstart their summer transfer business by agreeing a £35million deal with Atalanta to sign Ederson . There have been claims the deal may now be off - but the club are denying those reports, the Manchester Evening News understands United are also closing in on the signing of Andrey Santos from Chelsea in a deal worth £48million, plus a further £2million in add-ons Despite that, strengthening Michael Carrick's midfield remains the order of the day. The club decided against spending the lavish sums to sign Mateus Fernandes, Sandro Tonali and Elliot Anderson and are looking to be smarter in the market Alternatives won't come cheap, however. Real Madrid star Aurelien Tchouameni is said to be of interest, while Bournemouth have slapped an £80million valuation on Alex Scott Among the other players linked with Old Trafford are Sander Berge, Ayyoub Bouaddi and Felix Nmecha. Unsurprisingly, there will be no shortage of competition with the likes of Newcastle and Arsenal said to be in the market

title: Manchester United still haven't replaced one Casemiro trait they can't live without

input.lead: Man Utd aren't signing a natural replacement for Casemiro this summer, but they will need to look at replacing one attribute he brought to the side
input.summary: Man Utd aren't signing a natural replacement for Casemiro this summer, but they will need to look at replacing one attribute he brought to the side
input.evidence_text: Football Manchester United Transfer News Manchester United still haven't replaced one Casemiro trait they can't live without Man Utd aren't signing a natural replacement for Casemiro this summer, but they will need to look at replacing one attribute he brought to the side. How do you replace a five-time Champions League winner with nearly 500 appearances for Real Madrid and Manchester United and 91 caps for Brazil? For United, the answer is you don't replace him directly Casemiro has now formally left Old Trafford after his contract expired at the end of June, and the process of rebuilding the midfield has begun. His countryman Andrey Santos is set to sign for £50million, although a £38.3million deal for Ederson hangs in the balance Santos is just 22 and has limited senior experience, even if he has six caps for Brazil. Ederson's move from Atalanta could collapse over concerns around a knee injury, but although he is 27, his experience only amounts to playing for a top-six club in Serie A Get the best United news, updates and analysis straight to your Inbox courtesy of the Manchester Evening News! United's other primary midfield target at the moment is 22-year-old Alex Scott of Bournemouth, while they also wanted Elliot Anderson, 23, and Mateus Fernandes, 21, before they moved to Nottingham Forest and Tottenham, respectively

title: Андрей Кравченко

input.lead: Андрей Кравченко at Brixton Jamm
input.summary: Андрей Кравченко в Лондоне! 17 октября 2026 года в Brixton Jamm состоится концерт Андрея Кравченко в Лондоне
input.evidence_text: 17 октября 2026 года в Brixton Jamm состоится концерт Андрея Кравченко в Лондоне Андрей Кравченко покорил сердца тысяч слушателей благодаря своему уникальному голосу, харизме и способности превращать личные истории в песни, близкие каждому. В его творчестве смешиваются романтическая лирика, современное эстрадное звучание и проникновенная искренность После серии аншлаговых выступлений на самых престижных сценах Украины, среди которых Жовтневый Дворец и Дворец “Украина”, певец продолжает свое концертное путешествие и приглашает поклонников на особую встречу в Лондоне Концерты Андрея Кравченко известны своей теплой атмосферой: здесь нет дистанции между сценой и залом, ведь каждое выступление превращается в откровенный разговор на языке музыки. Гостей ждут любимые хиты, живой звук, море эмоций и моменты, которые захочется сохранить в памяти над Важной частью творческой миссии артиста остается поддержка Украины — часть средств от концерта будет передана на помощь ВСУ! Подарите себе вечер настоящей романтики, красивой музыки и искренних чувств вместе с Андреем Кравченко

final_reason: Без новых фактов: уже был 2026-06-26 как «Андрей Кравченко».

input.summary: Дронотур Бумбокса Європою. Листопад-грудень 2026 року
input.evidence_text: Дронотур Бумбокса Європою. Листопад-грудень 2026 року. Презентація максі-синглу "Живий" Кожного разу, коли перед менеджментом гурту Бумбокс стоїть завдання написати пресреліз, ми починаємо згадувати цікаві факти. Скільки концертів зіграли, скільки грошей зібрали, які пісні видали. Це база. Непогано ще пам’ятати, чи був гурт у країні, до якої ми збираємося, чи це пер О, як би хотілося написати: "Концерт гурту Бумбокс" — і як у Львові за добу продано 8000 квитків. Так-так, ми не про Львів розповідаємо, хоча теж про Європу Наші віддані слухачі, якщо ви перебуваєте за кордоном, сподіваємось, вам буде цікаво дізнатися, що після традиційної перерви на декілька місяців, Бумбокс поїде в турне від Ізраїлю до Німеччини, від Англії до Польщі, від Ірландії до Словаччини та Чехії І повеземо ми в це турне свіженький, як херсонський кавун, максі-сингл “Живий”, підсилений піснями з різних альбомів, під частину яких виросли ви, а під частину зростають ваші діти Тож ознайомтеся, будь ласка, уважно з розкладом туру, купуйте квитки заздалегідь, готуйте гроші на донати та мерч — зустрінемося в листопаді

input.summary: Economic Development Manchester United confirms new stadium location and plans for 'world-class' arena New 100,000-seater home will be some 350 metres away from Old Trafford Economic Development Tyrone Marshall Senior Manchester United Football Writer 08:32, 10 Jul 2026 Updated 0
input.evidence_text: Economic Development Manchester United confirms new stadium location and plans for 'world-class' arena New 100,000-seater home will be some 350 metres away from Old Trafford Economic Development Tyrone Marshall Senior Manchester United Football Writer 08:32, 10 Jul 2026 Updated 0 Manchester United have confirmed the club's new 100,000-seater stadium will be built on land recently purchased from Indurent, situated roughly 350 metres from Old Trafford United confirmed the location for the new stadium on Thursday in the Wharfside Strategic Masterplan and while the stadium won't be built on land currently connected to Old Trafford, they insist it will honour the current ground's history and traditions The new ground will be the centrepiece of a Stadium District, which will be purpose-built for sport, entertainment and year-round activity, with the new stadium serving as the flagship landmark of the wider Trafford Wharfside development The masterplan sets out a bold vision for Trafford Wharfside, including new and improved public transport links, enhanced rail connectivity, and extensive walking and cycling infrastructure. The vision is for a diverse neighbourhood creating 48,000 local jobs and 15,000 new homes It is estimated it could offer a £7.3bn per year boost to the UK economy, reports the Manchester Evening News

input.evidence_text: Greater Manchester's economic growth has not boosted outer borough incomes, report finds Greater Manchester's economic boom has not increased household incomes across outer boroughs, with earnings remaining stagnant despite regional growth, accordi Greater Manchester's relative economic success has failed to translate into improved wages or earnings for residents in its outer boroughs, according to a new report Research by Oxford Economics has indicated that "exceptional economic growth" has not resulted in higher earnings or income growth across the wider region Economists noted that elevated levels of economic inactivity and stagnant productivity gains in areas beyond Manchester city centre have constrained household finances The report scrutinises the broader narrative unfolding ahead of Andy Burnham's anticipated move into Number 10, with the former Greater Manchester mayor having claimed credit for the growth achievements recorded in recent years He has also leveraged strong growth figures to bolster his calls for greater devolution, while pledging "good growth in every postcode", as reported by City AM

It’s th Fauré wrote Cantique de Jean Racine as a nineteen-year-old entering a composition prize, and it’s remained a fixture of the choral repertoire ever since. James MacMillan’s O Radiant Dawn is unaccompanied, built from a medieval Advent text – the one clearly contemporary voice amon Elsewhere it’s louder again. Orff’s O Fortuna opens with a thunderous ostinato that gives the choir no room to breathe. Verdi’s Aida brings its Triumphal March and ballet music, full ceremony. Grieg’s Peer Gynt holds both extremes inside one work – the stillness of Morning agains Matthew Hamilton has run the Hallé Choir since 2015, and this kind of range – hushed part-songs sitting next to Orff at full volume – asks a lot of the full forces on stage: orchestra, choir and children’s choir together

UNITOM Projects – the new exhibition arm of UNITOM, the visual culture bookshop – launches in St John’s with a solo show by recent Manchester The 21-year-old painter’s work circles the Beat Generation: its street-level energy, its conviction that art and community and laughter matter more than productivity, its mythology of the city as a place of creative possibility Goof City takes its title from a concept coined by poet and activist Ed Sanders – introduced in his 1963 Poem from Jail – describing an imagined anarchic society built around art and community, historically associated with New York’s East Village. Lawless-Gill, who won the MAFA G Lawless-Gill describes her paintings as balancing “openness with concealment”, where meaning shifts between what is seen and what is remembered. input.evidence_text: Manchester’s award-winning theatre and arts charity 53two celebrates its 10 th birthday this month, marking the occasion with a programme of exciting events that aim to raise £10,000 to help secure the venue’s future Based within the Grade II-listed arches under the Manchester Central Convention Centre, the venue’s core ethos has been rooted in removing barriers and creating opportunities for artists and audiences across the region Now, the cultural hub is asking supporters for vital donations to help continue this work. Headline prizes during the fundraising month include the opportunity to appear as an extra on ITV’s Coronation Street and an exclusive one-of-a-kind original artwork of the iconic 53two arc The fundraising efforts will culminate on Sat 1 Aug with a star-studded Birthday Dinner Dance. The gala will feature a headline performance by legendary Manchester poet Tony Walsh, debuting a specially commissioned poem, These Bricks They Speak , and keynote reflections from BAFT Hosting the night will be renowned comedian and Manchester favourite Toby Hadoke, while Sing Their Name, a community choir for those bereaved by suicide, funded directly by 53two’s PMA grant programme, will be delivering a poignant performance Ahead of the gala, 53two will host a diverse mix of exciting events throughout July to bring people together and help raise at least £10,000
